title: ACDP Data Reference
description: A reference to a piece of data the context describes. Each data reference contains exactly one of 'location' (URI string or structured locator) or 'embedded' (inline content).

type.description: The role of this data within the context.

description.description: Human-readable description of the referenced data.

size_bytes.description: Size of the referenced or embedded data in bytes.

schema_version.description: Producer-specific schema version for this data.

content_hash.description: Optional SHA-256 hash for verifying data integrity at fetch time. For embedded data, computed over decoded bytes.

location.description: URI string or structured locator object identifying where the data resides.

oneOf.title: URI String Form
oneOf.description: URI with a valid scheme (RFC 3986). MUST NOT contain credentials in the userinfo component (the `user[:password]@` portion before the host). The body is signed and immutable, so secrets persisted in `location` cannot be redacted later. See RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.6 (DataRef validation checklist) and §10 (security considerations).

scheme.description: Dotted-namespace identifier for the locator type. Examples: kafka.offset, ipfs.cid, db.row, vendor.handle.

embedded.title: Embedded Content
embedded.description: Inline content for small data. Decoded size MUST NOT exceed 64 KB.

encoding.description: Encoding of the content. Determines how the content_hash is computed.

content.description: The embedded data. Type depends on encoding: any JSON value for 'json'; string for 'utf8' and 'base64'.

$comment: DataRef is an OPEN schema (additionalProperties: true). Root-level DataRef fields not defined in this RFC are producer-controlled and are included in ProducerContent (the content_hash preimage, RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.7) because each DataRef lives inside body.data_refs[]. Future ACDP minor versions MAY add producer-signed DataRef fields. Consumers MUST preserve unknown DataRef fields when recomputing content_hash; an implementation using typed structs MUST use an extension map (e.g. #[serde(flatten)] extensions) to avoid silently dropping fields the producer signed. The nested `embedded` sub-object is, by contrast, CLOSED. See RFC-ACDP-0002 §6.7 and the schema-openness map in RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3.1.
